atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain in the left elbow, particularly with flexion and extension.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports limited range of motion on extension of the left elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain with pronation of the right elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain with supination of the right elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain has been present for approximately a year and a half.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient states the pain began after lifting weights.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation over the medial aspect of the left elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Range of motion is limited on extension of the left elbow.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Sensation is intact to light touch in all dermatomes.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's symptoms and physical exam findings are consistent with medial epicondylitis (golfer's elbow).

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A cervical MRI is ordered to further evaluate the patient's elbow pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: We will also discuss a whole blood transfusion as a potential treatment option, pending the MRI results.
